properties.name: Grass Valley Library Royce Branch
properties.description: The Grass Valley Library - Royce Branch is a wonderfully historic Carnegie library listed in the National Register of Historic Places.

properties.name: KNCO / Star Radio
properties.description: Nevada County’s first choice for local news.

properties.description: Nevada Union has more to offer than any other comprehensive high school in the region. There is something here for everyone, and each provides an opportunity to shine. Whether it's academics, sports, dance, choir, AG metals, farming, drama, band, or woodworking, the list goes on.

properties.name: Sierra College
properties.description: Since its first days, the promise of Sierra College has been to facilitate learning, inspire change, and build community. The College continues this mission as it adapts to meet the ever changing needs of students while promoting learning as a lifelong process.

properties.name: The Book Seller
properties.description: We are a full-service independent bookstore located in historic downtown Grass Valley, the heart of California's Gold Country.

properties.name: The Union Newspaper
properties.description: The Union is is one of the oldest daily newspapers in California. It was founded in 1864, as the Civil War raged and people in Nevada County were divided. The Union made its stand clear: For The Union, One and Inseparable. Those words have appeared on its front page every day since then.

properties.name: Community Legal
properties.description: Community Legal, Inc. (CLI) is a non-profit organization located in Nevada City, California, that provides free and low-cost legal services to low-income residents of Nevada County.

properties.name: Economic Resource Council
properties.description: The NCERC is committed to ensuring a thriving, attractive business environment in Nevada County. We support scalable ventures that create quality jobs, promote entrepreneurship, help existing businesses, attract new businesses, and contribute to the overall prosperity of the community. Our organization provides a platform for dialogue and collaboration to create strong, informed strategies for Nevada County’s economic ecosystem.

properties.name: Friends of Nevada County Libraries
properties.description: Founded in 1972, Friends of Nevada County Libraries is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization dedicated to supporting and improving library services in Nevada County, California.

properties.name: Hardy Books
properties.description: Hardy Books is a rare and out-of-print book and ephemera seller located in Nevada City, California, in the heart of the California Northern Mines Gold Country. We specialize in Western Americana, "All Things Californian" (California History, Photographers, Poets, Authors, Law & Order, San Francisco, Yosemite, Monterey Peninsula, and so forth), Western Fine Press, Books to Film, Signed Books and Books On Books, as well as original Posters, Movie Memorabilia and the odd item of special interest or value. We list only a small part of our inventory on line, so please contact us if you are looking for a particular item.

properties.name: Harmony Books
properties.description: Nevada City's complete bookstore for all your reading enjoyment.

properties.name: Hour Nevada County
properties.description: Hour Nevada County is an hour exchange based on the equality of time, empowering individuals to utilize their assets and enhance their lives, neighborhood and community.

properties.name: KVMR
properties.description: KVMR provides community members opportunities to connect through the development and production of music, news, and public affairs programs that entertain, inform, and educate.

properties.name: Nevada City Chamber of Commerce
properties.description: The non-profit Nevada City Chamber of Commerce represents the interests of the local business community in Nevada City, California. The chamber is essential in fostering economic development, fighting for policies that are pro-business, and aiding small businesses in the area. The Nevada City Chamber of Commerce’s promotion of the community as a tourist and visitor destination is one of its main responsibilities. To develop marketing campaigns that highlight Nevada City’s distinctive culture, history, and natural beauty, the chamber collaborates closely with neighborhood businesses, lodging facilities, and tourist destinations.

properties.name: Nevada City Rotary Club
properties.description: The Nevada City Rotary Club is the service club people want to join because it’s a diverse and welcoming group of people who live Service Above Self by giving financial and hands-on support to our local, national and international communities, exemplify ethical leadership, and get things done while having good, clean fun.

properties.name: Nevada City School of the Arts
properties.description: We see our role as facilitators of the Arts in the community, enriching it with dance and theater, exhibitions and performances, collaborating with other artists, promoting and assisting the vision of arts-based education with other educational entities.

properties.name: Nevada County Digital Media Center
properties.description: The Nevada County Digital Media Center is Nevada County’s hub for all things film & video. Creation, Collaboration, Learning & Distribution!

properties.name: Madelyn Helling Library
properties.description: In August 1972, the Nevada County Library System was incorporated, with the Nevada City library serving as the main branch. Today the system includes the Grass Valley Library, the Truckee Library. the Doris Foley Library for Historical Research. The Bear River Library (a partnership with the Bear River High School Library) and Penn Valley Library (located in a shopping center) were added in 2002.

properties.name: Nevada County Television/Digital Media Ctr
properties.description: To foster community dialogue, increase civic engagement, and enrich the cultural life of our community through television, the internet, and other digital media.

properties.name: Searls Historical Library
properties.description: The Searls Historical Library is the archive of the Nevada County Historical Society. Established in 1973, the Searls Library has become one of the premier libraries of materials on the area's history, people and mines which contributed to the development of this county.

properties.name: Sierra FoodWineArt Magazine
properties.description: Sierra FoodWineArt is a free, quarterly lifestyle magazine that is the go-to publication for restaurants, wineries, wine & beer, arts & music, and travel & tourism in the Sierra Foothills, Tahoe/Truckee, Reno and outlying Sacramento region. We also have a companion website called SierraCulture.com.

properties.name: Sierra Harvest
properties.description: Sierra Harvest’s mission is to educate, inspire, and connect Nevada County families to fresh, local, seasonal food.

properties.name: The Center for Non Profit Leadership
properties.description: The Center for Nonprofit Leadership (CNL) is a resource center for organizations and individuals in Nevada County and the Sierra Nevada region of California. Founded in 2003 as a catalyst to strengthen and sustain local nonprofit organizations, CNL focuses on best practice workshops, executive leadership seminars, on-site coaching and mentoring, and public advocacy.

properties.name: KYRR 93.3 FM
properties.description: KYRR is a licensed non-profit public safety community radio station operated by Yuba River Radio, a community radio club, broadcasting 24/7 programs as diverse as heavy metal, trance, world, classic rock, punk, hip-hop, and pop music with a focus on local artists.

properties.name: Activities & Improvement Center
properties.description: Our Mission is to eliminate barriers to full equality for people with disabilities through programs which promote independent living and effect systems change, while honoring dignity and self determination.

properties.name: Penn Valley Chamber of Commerce
properties.description: Our mission is to promote and stimulate business growth and development to benefit area businesses and the communities they serve. To facilitate our mission, we are continually broadening our media outreach through community involvement and radio, TV, and newspaper coverage, in addition to application of the Internet and distribution of electronic media.

properties.name: Penn Valley Community Foundation
properties.description: Dedicated to establishing a Penn Valley Cultural Center, a centrally located facility for all ages and interests.

properties.name: Rough and Ready Chamber of Commerce
properties.description: Rough and Ready is located in Nevada County, California, just a short drive west of Grass Valley. Rough and Ready provides ideal country living with proximity to outdoor recreational activities, and yet we are reasonably close to metropolitan areas.

properties.name: Boys and Girls Club of North Lake Tahoe
properties.description: Our mission is to inspire and enable all youth, especially those who need us most, to reach their full potential as productive, caring, responsible citizens.

properties.name: Community Collaborative of Tahoe Truckee
properties.description: The Community Collaborative of Tahoe Truckee (CCTT) is a partnership of nonprofit and public organizations working together to address fundamental needs of families in the Tahoe Truckee Region. Collectively, we identify emerging community issues and develop strategies with our combined vision and resources.

properties.name: Family Resource Center of Truckee
properties.description: To promote social and economic success in our community by providing education, mobilizing resources and advocating for change.

properties.name: Friends of the Truckee Library
properties.description: Friends of the Truckee Library is a nonprofit organization dedicated to promoting and providing support for the Truckee Library as an essential institution of society. Our members advocate, educate and raise funds on behalf of the library. We are a dedicated group of active volunteers that have spent decades devoted to growing and sustaining the Truckee Library.

properties.name: Grant Kaye Photography
properties.description: I lead small (less than 6) groups of keen photographers on guided workshops to destinations around the globe. My trips are custom tailored to offer the perfect balance of adventure, photography, and learning

properties.name: High Fives Foundation
properties.description: The High Fives Foundation supports the dreams of outdoor action sports athletes by raising injury prevention awareness while providing resources and inspiration to those who suffer life altering injuries.

properties.name: Kitchen Collab
properties.description: We are a collaborative kitchen space and culinary incubator available for rent to caterers, private chefs, food production companies, food trucks, food educators, and health professionals. Our Mission is to elevate and strengthen the Truckee Tahoe food economy by supporting its food entrepreneurs.

properties.name: KTKE Radio 101.5 FM
properties.description: Our Mission is to entertain and inform the local community. We are independent so we can play and talk about what you want to hear. Our format is Triple A, which means we play a wide variety of music.

properties.name: Moonshine Ink
properties.description: Moonshine Ink brings you Tahoe news straight from the source. Our team of hardworking journalists uncovers North Tahoe and Truckee news stories with in depth reporting and our authentic for the community, by the community spirit.

properties.name: Sierra Business Council
properties.description: Sierra Business Council pioneers and demonstrates innovative approaches and solutions to increase community vitality, economic prosperity, environmental quality, and social fairness in the Sierra Nevada.

properties.name: Tahoe Donner Homeowners Association
properties.description: Tahoe Donner is a recreational oriented mountain residential community whose mutual benefit association of 6,500 owners provides for the standards, regular operation and long term maintenance of programs, facilities and open space.

properties.name: Tahoe Safe Alliance
properties.description: Our mission is to end the incidence and trauma of intimate partner and domestic violence, sexual violence, and child abuse in North Lake Tahoe and Truckee.

properties.name: Tahoe Truckee Community Foundation
properties.description: Tahoe Truckee Community Foundation connects people and opportunities, generating resources to build a more caring, creative, and effective community.

properties.name: Town of Truckee
properties.description: Creating the best future for our community requires the highest level of public service every day. We will accomplish that in partnership with the Town’s citizens and our fellow governmental agencies.

properties.name: Truckee Chamber of Commerce
properties.description: Our mission is to promote and develop local business and tourism while improving the community's quality of life.

properties.name: Truckee Downtown Merchants Association
properties.description: The vision of Truckee Downtown Merchants Association (TDMA) is to create, support and sustain a vibrant and visible downtown through solid membership, community collaboration and unique events; ensuring a vital, art-filled and welcoming authentic Historic Downtown Truckee for residents and visitors alike.

properties.name: Truckee Tahoe Community Television
properties.description: The mission of Truckee Tahoe Community Television is to provide a PEG Access Television Center to the residents of Truckee and North Tahoe, CA at limited cost.

properties.name: Word after Word Books
properties.description: Word After Word Books is a welcoming space for the community and visitors to connect around books. The Word After Word Team hosts author events, writing workshops, book clubs, and story times.

properties.name: Truckee Tahoe Community Television (TTCTV)
properties.description: At Truckee Tahoe Community Television our mission is to foster community awareness and communication through local programming from individuals, groups and agencies. We serve the Truckee, North Tahoe area with interesting and informative programs through Suddenlink Channel 6, Channel 18 and online video streaming. We instruct the general public, and TTUSD students in particular, in the motion picture arts and sciences.

properties.name: 4th of July Parade
properties.description: The 4th of July celebration and parade alternates each year between downtown Grass Valley and downtown Nevada City.

properties.name: 4th of July Celebration Fireworks
properties.description: Following the parade, there will be a celebration under the pines at the Nevada County Fairgrounds. Live music, entertainment, fireworks, food and refreshments are offered.

properties.name: Celtic Festival
properties.description: First founded in 1996, the KVMR Celtic Festival includes eleven performance areas including five musical and six demonstration stages and a weekend long youth arts instruction that has included Celtic music, magic, dance and songwriting.

properties.name: Cornish Christmas
properties.description: Cornish Christmas started in 1967 as a way to preserve Grass Valley’s Cornish heritage and holiday traditions. Mill and West Main Streets are closed to motorized traffic and filled with the sights and sounds of an old fashioned Christmas; carolers, jugglers, musicians, magicians, the Grass Valley Cornish Carol Choir, Tommyknocker Cloggers and of course, Santa Claus. Enjoy delicious food and drink while perusing handmade arts and crafts from artists throughout California.

properties.name: Country Christmas Craft Fair
properties.description: Grass Valley hosts a major Country Christmas Faire at the Nevada County Fairgrounds with 100+ artisans, Santa, bonfire, music, and crafts.

properties.name: Cruzin' in the Pines Car Show
properties.description: One of the best classic car shows in Northern California.

properties.name: Draft Horse Classic
properties.description: The Classic has grown to become the premier Draft Horse show in the western United States, and features six majestic performances, breathtaking competitions and entertainment in between classes.

properties.name: Earth's Treasures Gem & Mineral Show
properties.description: Check out the gem and mineral collections, visit the booths of the numerous vendors, bid on minerals and rocks at a silent auction, watch local artisans at work, and see the demonstrations. This show is a great place to meet other enthusiasts, buy supplies, and experience everything the rock and mineral hobby has to offer.

properties.name: Fall Colors Open Studio Tour (host:Center for the Arts)
properties.description: The Center for the Arts, with support from the Nevada County Arts Council, presents the Fall Colors Open Studio Tour. Over 70 local artists welcome the public to 47 studios and art galleries during a free tour spanning two weekends in October.

properties.name: Fathers Day Bluegrass Festival
properties.description: California Bluegrass Association presents a legendary bluegrass, old time, and gospel music festival over four days in June.

properties.name: Foothills Celebration
properties.description: The Foothills Celebration is just the therapy you’ll need for a late winter weekend. The wineries and restaurants are all paired with local downtown Grass Valley merchants who open their stores for this unique wine and food tasting affair.

properties.name: Grass Valley Car Show
properties.description: Travel back in time and experience American nostalgia at its best. See approximately 300 of the finest custom, vintage and antique cars and trucks in all of Northern California; in a city that honors its historic past and embraces its wonderful future: Historic Downtown Grass Valley.

properties.name: Grass Valley Farmers Market
properties.description: The Grass Valley Thursday Night Markets are a wonderful community event that includes a certified Farmer’s Market and a whole lot more including arts, crafts, food, and the ever popular FREE Summer Concert Series.

properties.name: Grass Valley Old West Antique Show
properties.description: The annual Old West Antiques Show provides the opportunity to view and purchase world class merchandise from a legendary list of dealers in all collecting categories, including museum quality Antique Firearms, Native American items, Western & Cowboy Memorabilia, Advertising, Coin Operated & Gambling, Saloon & General Store, Paintings, Bronzes, Lamps, Gas & Oil, Jewelry, Furniture and more!

properties.name: Holidays at the Empire Mine
properties.description: Celebrate the holidays early 1900s’ style at Holidays at Empire Mine. With old fashioned decorations, lavish period costumes, and festive live music, Empire Cottage will be brimming with good cheer. Docents will share insights about Empire’s glory days when it ranked as one of the most prosperous hard-rock gold mines in North American history

properties.name: Miners Picnic
properties.description: The annual Miners Picnic is a summer celebration of our colorful, gold mining history. The first Miners Picnic was held in 1895 to raise money for miners’ widows and orphans, as well as injured and out of work miners. Today, it’s an action packed day of fun, food, gold history, and lasting memories.

properties.name: Music in the Mountains Summer Fest
properties.description: For more than 37 years, Music in the Mountains SummerFest has been Northern California’s premier summer concert series, featuring exciting outdoor performances and sophisticated chamber concerts, demonstrating that classical music, performed by a world class orchestra and chorus, can transform lives and communities.

properties.name: Nevada County Certified Growers Market
properties.description: Nevada County's original certified market, bringing growers and the community together since 1980.

properties.name: Nevada County Fair
properties.description: Five days of excitement and fun for Fair goers of all ages. Enjoy live entertainment, delicious food, carnival rides, animals, and exhibits in a community friendly environment and at affordable family prices!

properties.name: Sierra BrewFest
properties.description: A unique, unlimited microbrew tasting experience. Sample over 60 microbrews and a no host wine bar for those who would prefer wine over beer! This year features even more delicious food from some of the best local food trucks and restaurants.

properties.name: Sierra Poetry Festival
properties.description: In addition to its mainstage event, Sierra Poetry Festival marks National Poetry Month from the rolling foothills of California’s Gold Country to the rugged High Sierra, bringing our rich literary community together to celebrate the spoken word and reach out to brand new audiences in fresh ways. Readings, workshops, music, an activity fair for all ages and youth performances will be preceded by a month of pre festival pop-up poetry events.

properties.name: St. Piran's Day Festival
properties.description: Every year Grass Valley captures some fun with its annual St. Piran’s Day pasty tossing contest. The rain or shine event, which celebrates the Cornish immigrants who settled around the gold mines, is fun for adults, children and especially dogs.

properties.name: Strawberry Music Festival
properties.description: The Strawberry Music Festival presents the gold standard in main stage entertainment, intimate side stage performances, top notch children’s programming, workshops and activities for the whole family, and some of the nicest people you will ever meet. Programs like the Breakfast Club and the Band Scramble, live broadcasts by Hog Ranch Radio, and Strawberry’s legendary camp jam culture are what makes this your festival.

properties.name: Chinese New Year Festival & Parade
properties.description: The Festival and Parade honors the early Chinese pioneers of the Sierra.

properties.name: Constitution Day Parade
properties.description: Nevada City’s Constitution Day Parade, a local tradition since 1967, is reported to be the oldest and largest Constitution observance in western America. The parade through the downtown historic district includes marching bands, floats, antique autos, equestrians, politicians and the perennial crowd favorites the Famous Marching Presidents of Nevada City.

properties.name: First Friday Art Walk
properties.description: The First Friday Art Walk is a celebration of Nevada City’s diverse mix of galleries, artisan boutiques, retail shops, wine tasting rooms, restaurants, as well as our wonderfully artistic and creative community.

properties.name: Mardis Gras Parade
properties.description: The 27th anniversary of Nevada City’s annual Mardi Gras Celebration is set to happen on Sunday, February 17, 2019. The annual event, hosted by the Nevada City Chamber of Commerce, features a colorful & festive parade & street faire throughout the streets of historic downtown Nevada City.

properties.name: Nevada City Craft Fair
properties.description: Nevada City Craft Fair is a juried exhibition of makers curated in the aesthetics of the modern rustic lifestyle that NCCF embodies. Hip, sustainable, yet innovative, represents the best of the region in our bi annual craft fair held at the historic Miners Foundry in downtown Nevada City.

properties.name: Nevada City Farmers Market
properties.description: Each Saturday from June to November more than 30 vendors join a group of loyal volunteers to create a vibrant community event on Union Street in downtown Nevada City.

properties.name: Nevada City Film Festival
properties.description: NCFF screens almost 100 films, including comedies, dramas, documentaries, animation, and international shorts, in a variety of historic locations in downtown Nevada City. By combining creative programming of short and full length films, enthusiastic audiences and a truly festive environment, NCFF has become known as one of the most innovative, fun and artist friendly events on the film festival circuit.

properties.name: Nevada City Summer Nights
properties.description: The Summer Nights in Nevada City celebration, a street festival of art and music, has been an annual tradition in this quaint, scenic, historic (and sometimes quirky) community, for close to 30 years. This free event takes place on three Wednesday evenings in July, when Nevada City’s landmark historic district is closed to motorized traffic and filled with arts, crafts, classic cars, food, drink and music. Leading Sierra foothills musicians perform on outdoor stages throughout the downtown area.

properties.name: The Childrens’ Festival
properties.description: The Children’s Festival has become one of the premiere summer events for children, with over 2000 children and adults attending. All who attend leave with fond memories and many of the children who attended in the 1970’s and 80’s are now returning with their own children. A very special event that brings the community together to celebrate the magic of imagination.

properties.name: Victorian Christmas
properties.description: The enticing aromas of roasted chestnuts and hearty holiday foods fill the air, along with cries of street vendors hawking their wares and lamp lit streets filled to overflowing with handcrafted candy, jewelry, pottery, perfume, dolls, and over 100 different items in all. This annual, family tradition takes place 2nd Wednesday evenings and 3rd Sunday afternoons in December and features holiday activities for all ages.

properties.name: Wild & Scenic Environmental Film Festival
properties.description: One of the nation’s premier environmental and adventure film festivals, the 17th annual event will feature films that combine stellar filmmaking, beautiful cinematography and first-rate storytelling to inform, inspire and ignite solutions and possibilities to restore the earth. Each year, the Wild & Scenic Film Festival draws top filmmakers, celebrities, leading activists, social innovators and well-known world adventurers for film, music, workshops, celebrations, and inspiration.

properties.name: Sierra Storytelling Festival
properties.description: Known as the Queen of Storytelling Festivals, our festival brings a lineup of talented, nationally recognized storytellers to the Schoolhouse stages. People have told stories since the beginning of time and our storytellers keep the tradition alive, voicing the narratives of life, our histories and traditions, our reasons for living, our hopes and dreams.

properties.name: Penn Valley Rodeo
properties.description: PVCRA produces quality family entertainment in the western tradition with two nights of pro rodeo, Friday and Saturday. Events include AMA sanctioned Mule & Donkey Show, the Doc Halstead Open Barrel Race series, and Summer Fun Gymkhana Buckle series.

properties.name: Secession Days
properties.description: On the first Sunday of June, Rough and Ready celebrates Secession Days with a special Chili Cookoff. Guests can taste and vote for their favorite chili along with the judges. The Original Saga of Rough and Ready melodrama, Blacksmith demonstrations in the historic Fippin Blacksmith Shop, a variety of vendors and crafters, music, additional food options from the local Community Hall and Firefighter's Association, raffles, souvenir sales, water slide and bounce house for the kids...it's fun for the whole family!

properties.name: Art Hike
properties.description: A 3-mile art hike combining dance, poetry, music, storytelling and environmental art with family, leisure and meditative hikes.

properties.name: New Years Eve Light Parade & Fireworks
properties.description: Let's ring in the New Year in mountain fashion. This free fun event is for intermediate skiers and riders ages 10 or older who can ski or ride unassisted in the dark on the Snowbird run. Come early to secure a spot in the parade. If you are wanting to spectate the event, join us at the bar for a warm drink. The New Year’s Fireworks show will follow the Light Parade.

properties.name: Summer Music Series
properties.description: Every Wednesday from June 20 to August 29, TDRPD hosts a community gathering with great music in the amphitheater located at Truckee Regional Park.

properties.name: Truckee Rodeo
properties.description: The Truckee Professional Rodeo Association strives to enrich our community by keeping the western heritage of our town alive and by giving back to other local organizations each year.

properties.name: Truckee Thursdays
properties.description: Truckee Thursdays is a feel good event that beckons the community to enjoy some exuberant downtown summertime energy. Offering a stage with live music; a food court with an eclectic selection of eats; children’s activities; a beer garden; and a wide variety of artisan vendors. In addition, attendees will find sidewalk sales and other activities hosted by downtown merchants.

properties.name: Art Works Gallery Co-op
properties.description: Located in the historic gold mining town of Grass Valley, California, the gallery is at the center of a vibrant visual and performing arts community. Over 30 juried visual artists cooperate in presenting their work in this 19th century building. The work includes ceramic, glass, jewelry, painting, woodturning, furniture, fiber art, metal, mosaic, mixed media, photography, and sculpture.

properties.name: Artists Studio in the Foothills (ASIF)
properties.description: The Artists Studio in the Foothills (ASiF) is a 4000 sq. ft., fully-equipped studio art center. In 2008 ASiF opened its doors with a mission to nurture the creative spirit of the community, offer a professional studio atmosphere and creative home for visual artists by providing a place where are can be made, displayed and appreciated. The center offers classes and workshops in a variety of media for all ages, private and shared studio space for working artists, and a gallery to promote local and visiting artists.

properties.name: BriarPatch Art Gallery and Amphitheater
properties.description: BriarPatch Food Co-op offers art exhibit space for the enjoyment of our customers and to promote local art.

properties.name: Granucci Gallery
properties.description: This aesthetically pleasing display space for the visual arts features shows by local, regional, and international artists. The gallery also serves as an intimate venue for meetings, artist receptions, literary and dramatic readings. The space includes a concessions area and no-host bar.

properties.name: Ellu Gallery
properties.description: Ellu Gallery is a 2,200 sq. ft. artist owned collaborative gallery and event space. Ellu provides custom staging, commercial art lease programs and rents the gallery space for truly unique events and custom shows. Both private and public events are available. Hand selected artists versed in large scale one of a kind paintings and sculptures are featured at Ellu. Life is Art. Ellu Gallery breaks the old paradigm into a timeless lucid dream with an ever expanding collection from Zen surrealism, abstract, Expressionism, blown glass, wood sculpture, to hand made guitars.

properties.name: St. Joseph Cultural Center
properties.description: St. Joseph's Cultural Center is a nonprofit organization dedicated to preserving a historic landmark for cultural activities in the community. It is owned and operated by the Historic Mount St. Mary's Preservation Committee.

properties.name: Lucchesi Vineyards Tasting Room
properties.description: The Lucchesi Tasting Room is open from 11:00 am until the party's over, every day except Easter, Christmas, and Thanksgiving. Our summer hours are extended for special events, please call us for more information.

properties.name: North Star House
properties.description: Our mission is to rehabilitate and transform the Historic North Star House into a premier cultural event center and to promote Nevada County’s legacy by fostering local arts, letters and sciences.

properties.name: North Star Power House & Pelton Wheel Museum
properties.description: The North Star Mining Museum, a part of the Nevada County Historical Society, is one of the gems of Nevada County and exists not only to preserve and protect our area’s mining heritage but also educates and informs people not only about the mining process, but also about the impact the industry had upon the humanity of the people who built the mines and those who toiled there during their operation.

properties.name: Starr Winery Tasting Room
properties.description: Sierra Starr Vineyard and Winery is a small vineyard and winery in the Sierra Foothills AVA. Owned and operated by the Starr Family, Phil and Anne since 1995 and their son Jackson since 2005.

properties.name: Swan Levine House & Printmaking Studio
properties.description: The Swan Levine House plays significantly into the history of Nevada County. The fine art of Printmaking is illustrated by the extensive collection of art throughout the house.

properties.name: The Artist Workshop
properties.description: We offer Pottery, Candle Making, Paint & Sips, Wood Signs, DIY Bags and Pillows and Canvas Art, we also have a great retail section with art supplies. We are a place to come to make gifts, unique pieces for your home or just a place to come and get crafty.

properties.name: The Louvre Framing, Restoration and Gallery
properties.description: Locally owned and operated, we provide top quality custom framing and art restoration. While you're in, visit our on-site gallery.

properties.name: West Wind
properties.description: A Garden Sanctuary Store featuring local artists Deborah Bridges, Jerrianne Van Dyk, Meg Black Smith, Jerry Delong and many others.

properties.name: Wild Eye Pub
properties.description: Wild Eye Pub gives customers great food, inspired drinks, and attentive service in a comfortable, art-filled space; a perfect place to connect with others, nurture creative ideas, and nourish body and spirit.

properties.name: Stucki Jewelers and Engravers
properties.description: Stucki Jewelers specializes in every aspect of fine jewelry. We have a qualified and caring sales staff to help you find the perfect gift for that special loved one or that special something you have always wanted. We also check and clean your jewelry complimentary, and offer a full service, onsite repair shop for all your repair needs. By supporting Stucki Jewelers you support us in giving back to our local community, as we continue to donate to more than 45 local organizations

properties.name: Elixart Herbal Lounge & Gallery
properties.description: Elixart is an herbal elixir lounge and visionary art gallery offering the highest integrity and highest vibration ingredients throughout our menu of mood-enhancing elixir drinks, kava, tea, and other exotics; raw food, and premium organic products.

properties.name: Firehouse No.1
properties.description: Completed in the Spring of 1861, this firehouse was home to Nevada Hose Company No.1 from 1861 to 1938. During those nearly 80 years, horse -drawn fire wagons, handcarts and “modern” motorized fire engines rolled out the doors.

properties.name: Grass Roots Antique Tool Museum
properties.description: Our specialty is one-of-a-kind custom installations designed virtually from photos of your home, office or business.

properties.name: Kitkitdizzi
properties.description: It's pronounced: kit-kid-dizzy, but without heavily spacing or over annunciating those four syllables. It's a native plant. Bear Clover, Mountain Misery or Kitkitdizze, as the Miwok referred to it, is endemic to the western side of the Sierra Nevada. If you've hiked in these woods and haven't noticed it underfoot, it's resinous leaves adhering to your pants and shoes, you've most certainly taken in the scent of this highly aromatic little fernlike plant carpeting the forest floor. It is essential to the health of the forests in these parts and for many of us, is the smell of home.

properties.name: LeeAnn Brook Gallery
properties.description: Opened in July 2015, LeeAnn Brook Fine Art is one of the first artist-owned gallery and on site studio in Nevada County, California. Located at 231 Broad St, in the heart of historic downtown Nevada City, the gallery also incorporates the working studio of LeeAnn Brook, where visitors can see paintings in progress and discuss the art with the artist firsthand.

properties.name: Lilly Vigil Gallery
properties.description: Lilly Vigil Gallery is located within the heart of the Sierra Nevada in beautiful downtown Nevada City, California. Since 1985, Vigil's is recognized for unparalleled quality of artistic and cultural treasures, focusing on fine Native American art. The gallery also specializes in originals, graphics, sculptors and exquisite jewelry from local, regional, and national artists.

properties.name: Narrow Gauge Railroad Museum
properties.description: Located in Nevada City, California, the Nevada County Narrow Gauge Railroad and Transportation Museum is dedicated to the preservation of local transportation history and artifacts from the narrow gauge railroad era. Visitors are offered a docent led historical tour of the museum, rail yard, and restoration shop. There is no admission charge.

properties.name: Nevada City Picture Framing
properties.description: Our mission is to provide you, the customer, with an enjoyable full spectrum experience at our shop, whether in custom framing, art restoration, or fine art acquisition. Our friendly staff of experts is always ready to help you create a one of a kind, unique frame design. Stop by our gallery for art and gifts!

properties.name: Searls Historical Library (NC Historical Society)
properties.description: The Searls Historical Library is the archive of the Nevada County Historical Society. Established in 1973, the Searls Library has become one of the premier libraries of materials on the area's history, people and mines which contributed to the development of this county.

properties.name: Spirithouse Import Gallery
properties.description: Featuring a fairly traded, personally chosen collection of fine artisan jewelry, clothing and home decor, from the Hearts and Hands of Southeast Asia

properties.name: The Curious Forge
properties.description: Our mission is to provide access to traditional and advanced tools, a large space and a variety of skill sets in a collaborative and innovative environment that enables the members to create what matters to them.

properties.name: Utopian Stone
properties.description: Nestled in the Sierra Nevada Foothills, Utopian Stone was founded in 1974 by G. Patrick Dyer and wife, Diane. We employ four master goldsmiths, plus a fifth, Dean Dussell, who works from his studio in Colorado. At Utopian Stone we specialize in high quality one of a kind work and customer commissions. Our favorite thing is to design for clients who have no idea what they want. As we do not reproduce our one of a kind creations, our customers challenge our designers who may want something similar to a one of a kind piece, but with its own unique personal flair

properties.name: Nevada City Crystal and Glass
properties.description: Swarovski Crystal Living, Swarovski Jewelry, Anna Leece Jewelry, Art Glass, Home Decor, Barware, Custom-made Jewelry, Shopping.

properties.name: Western SkiSport Museum
properties.description: The Western SkiSport Museum was founded in 1969 by the Auburn Ski Club. Working with ski writer and historian William B. Berry, the Museum was developed as an exhibition of Western North American ski history. Today the Museum continues on a mission to collect, preserve and exhibit the history of winter ski sports in the western United States.

properties.name: Art Truckee
properties.description: Art Gallery, Performance and Event Venue and Wine Bar

properties.name: Atelier & Bespoke
properties.description: Founded in 2015, Atelier offers a collection of hand-sourced 2D prints, paintings and designer art wares and an exclusive collection of artist supplies and materials. Atelier strives to be the creative hub of the local art community by hosting workshops taught by the area’s most sought after artists, illustrators, designers, weavers, knitters, painters and more who share the vision to create always and inspire all.

properties.name: Emigrant Trail Museum - Donner Lake Memorial State Park - Museum
properties.description: The Emigrant Trail Museum contains exhibits about the cultural history of the area, including local Native Americans, the Donner Party, and building of the First Transcontinental Railroad.

properties.name: Gallery 5830’
properties.description: Gallery 5830′ is a creative portal, featuring an eclectic mix of artwork and furniture, hand crafted by artists in the local community and neighboring regions. Through our mission to connect clients with the creative community, Gallery 5830’ offers a selection of in house artwork for sale on consignment. Gallery 5830’ also facilitates artist/ client commissions that emphasize a collaborative vision, site-specific designs and refined old-world craftsmanship.

properties.name: Joseph Research Cabin
properties.description: Truckee Donner Historical Society was founded July 24, 1968 to preserve the history of the Truckee Donner area. Besides maintaining the historic Joseph Research Cabin (the Gateway Cabin), the Society is also responsible for operating the Old Jail Museum which was built in 1875. The Society maintains the Community Memorial Garden including the Memorial Arch behind the Old Jail Museum, the Donner Cross, and Schaffer Cemetery.

properties.name: Lorien Powers Studio Jewelry
properties.description: Lorien Powers is an artist who specializes in creating industrial and contemporary style jewelry. Lorien prefers to work with gold, silver, natural gems, and minerals. Known for the decorative features of her work, she employs a wide variety of techniques, including roller printing, cold connecting, riveting, fabricating and forging. She draws inspiration for her jewelry from many different sources, some from nature, some from music (Phish in particular), some from industrial environments.

properties.name: Mountain Arts Collective
properties.description: Located at the Train Depot in historic Downtown Truckee, Mountain Arts Collective began in 2017, with three local artists who wanted to showcase quality art by individuals closely connected to the community. Our goal is to create a welcoming space that will attract locals and visitors with high end statement pieces, more affordable arts & gifts, and everything in between.

properties.name: Mountain Forge
properties.description: Hand forged iron work.

properties.name: Old Jail Museum
properties.description: Truckee’s historic jail museum is one of only a few surviving 19th-century jailhouses of its kind in the West and one of the few remaining original buildings in Truckee. The old Bastille was used continuously from 1875 until May 1964, and ably withstood the many fires that swept the town in its early days. Although jails are usually sturdy, Truckee’s is a virtual strongbox. The original building consisted of just the lower level, constructed of native stone. The walls are 32-inches thick at the lower level, with no windows unless one counts the small vents for each cell, which are set with irregular rows of two-inch steel bars. The ceilings are plate steel, insulated with dirt, and lined with narrow gauge railroad tracks. All doors are riveted steel, weighing an estimated 200 pounds each.

properties.name: Riverside Studios
properties.description: Riverside Studios mission is to cultivate artistic growth in the Truckee Community while handcrafting one of a kind artistic goods, creating a unique shopping experience for both locals and tourists alike. From paintings to pottery, from leather goods to jewelry and metal sculpture there is something for everyone.

properties.name: Truckee Railroad Museum
properties.description: Truckee Railroad Museum is located next to the historic railroad station in downtown Truckee. This adds historical significance to the area, as the caboose is from the Southern Pacific Railroad and was used in the area. Paint for the caboose matches the original SP paint scheme. Inside the museum are stories, pictures, recreations, and railroad artifacts depicting the impact of railroads in the formation and development of Truckee. The First Transcontinental Railroad, Logging Railroads, and Tourism by Rail all played an important part in Truckee’s history.

properties.name: Truckee Tahoe Airport District (Art at the Airport)
properties.description: Since 1958, the Truckee Tahoe Airport District has been providing a high quality, safe facility for pilots, visitors and the community. Nestled in the Sierra Nevada mountain range at an elevation of 5,900 ft., twelve miles from Lake Tahoe, we promise one of the most scenic flight and visiting experiences in the world. We are proud of our pilot safety and community programs, state of the art energy saving facilities and top notch staff and leadership. We are committed to offering the best small, mountain airport in the Country.

properties.name: Center for the Arts
properties.description: The Center for the Arts is a cultural and educational organization that promotes and presents the literary, visual, and performing arts for the enrichment of our community.

properties.name: Del Oro Theater
properties.description: Sierra Theaters is a locally owned, family business that operates Sierra Cinemas, The Del Oro Theatre, Sutton Cinemas, and The Nevada Theatre Film Series. Each theatre features comfortable seats, friendly staff, and state-of-the-art projection and sound. The popcorn is delicious, too!

properties.name: Don Baggett Theater
properties.description: Have an Interesting presentation coming up? How about an important conference? The Don Baggett Theatre Is the place for you. Our facilities Provide with access to a theatre, our own in house technicians and use of our scene shop/green room.

properties.name: Grass Valley Veterans Memorial Hall
properties.description: This treasured historic venue is the meeting and special events facility for veterans groups including the American Legion Post No.130, Disabled American Veterans, Marine Corps League, and the Vietnam Veterans. As a service to the community this facility is available to rent year around. This multipurpose venue was renovated in 2008 to provide modern comfort while preserving its historic character.

properties.name: Lucchesi Vineyards & Winery
properties.description: We specialize in traditionally crafted wines produced in small lots to capture their varietal character and regional qualities. We are currently growing 16 different varietals at our winery in Grass Valley including Zinfandel, Merlot, Chardonnay, Sauvignon Blanc Muscat, Sauvignon Blanc, Pinot Grigio, Syrah, Cabernet Franc, Viognier, Cabernet Sauvignon, Tempranillo, Petite Sirah, and Petit Verdot, and three Portuguese Port Varietals: Tinto Cão, Tinta Negra Molé, and Touriga Nacional. We hope you come out for wine tasting at the Vineyard.

properties.name: Nevada County Fairgrounds
properties.description: Located in Grass Valley, California, the Nevada County Fairgrounds has been declared as California's Most Beautiful Fairgrounds, and is covered with hundreds of towering pine trees on almost 90 acres. It is home to the annual Nevada County Fair, the Draft Horse Classic, the Country Christmas Faire, world-class music festivals, and year-round community events. There is also a year-round RV Park, Lions Lake, rental facilities, weekly events, weddings, some of our community's largest outdoor events, and lots of areas to walk.

properties.name: North Star House
properties.description: The North Star House is a 10,000 square foot mansion, built in the California Craftsman style in 1905. The North Star House is listed on the National and State Registers of Historic Places. It is located on a portion of the former North Star Mine, a 14-acre site of spectacular Gold Country landscapes. The North Star House is owned, operated and being lovingly restored by the North Star Historic Conservancy, a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization.

properties.name: Off Center Stage
properties.description: Our professionally equipped studio theater located at the back of our building at 315 Richardson Street offers an intimate space suitable for a wide variety of performances and events.

properties.name: Prime Cinemas Sutton
properties.description: Sierra Theaters is a locally owned, family business that operates Sierra Cinemas, The Del Oro Theatre, Sutton Cinemas, and The Nevada Theatre Film Series. Each theatre features comfortable seats, friendly staff, and state-of-the-art projection and sound.æ The popcorn is delicious, too!

properties.name: Miners Foundry Cultural Center
properties.description: The Miners Foundry Cultural Center is a living historic treasure serving Nevada County as a cultural arts and community events center. More than a venue, the nonprofit's mission is to preserve, enhance and utilize the historic Miners Foundry for cultural, educational and social activities in Nevada County.

properties.name: Nevada Theatre
properties.description: The Nevada Theatre is the oldest existing theatre building on the west coast that continues to operate as a theatre. We are now celebrating 152 years of history, live performances, music, movies and art in Nevada County.

properties.name: North Columbia Schoolhouse Cultural Center
properties.description: The North Columbia Schoolhouse Cultural Center gives the rural San Juan Ridge community a local place to meet, to share its creativity, and to participate in a wide variety of enriching activities. Located 20 miles north of Nevada City, the Schoolhouse was built by the mining community in 1875, and has a long tradition of serving the community as a school and public meeting hall.

properties.name: Off Broadstreet Theatre
properties.description: We produce only comedies, and most of these are musicals, with a slant toward familiar hit songs of the 1950's and 60's. Intimate cabaret seating and a relaxed and friendly atmosphere add to the uniqueness of the experience. Also, we provide a modestly priced selection of refreshments, including locally produced wines, desserts, coffee and other beverages, served at your table before the show and during intermission.

properties.name: Pioneer Park Bandshell
properties.description: The Bandshell is designed for acoustic music and is therefore a nice location for an event offered by a concert band or small music event. Amplified music is discouraged at this location. The Bandshell is also a nice location for a family & friends gathering. Power, water and private bathrooms are available at this location.

properties.name: The Onyx Theater
properties.description: The Onyx Theatre is dedicated to bringing the very best in independent, foreign language, and documentary film to our region. We make a conscious commitment to screen works from underrepresented communities and alternative perspectives; fostering understanding and the creative spirit.

properties.name: Shady Creek Events Center
properties.description: Shady Creek Outdoor School and Conference Center is a 132-acre property located in the Sierra Nevada Foothills just outside of Nevada City in California's Gold Country.

properties.name: Art Truckee
properties.description: Art Gallery, Performance and Event Venue, Wine Bar

properties.name: Community Arts Center
properties.description: Our auditorium at the CAC is well-equipped for your next public or private event. Features include a large stage, theatrical lighting, and live audio production equipment. You can also project films or other media content to a full-size cinema screen, the only one in Truckee!

properties.name: McIver Arena
properties.description: The McIver Rodeo Arena hosts the annual Truckee Rodeo featuring the Truckee Donner Horsemen. It is also available to rent for a variety of horse-friendly uses.

properties.name: Salty Gebhardt Amphitheater -Truckee River Park
properties.description: Set near the Truckee River, just across the Legacy Trail, lies The Salty Gebhart Amphitheater. An outdoor entertainment venue with comfortable lawn seating overlooks a generous stage for all sorts of performances. It's also the home of our annual Wednesday Summer Music Series! Popular uses include weddings, concerts, and plays.

properties.name: The Village Cinemas
properties.description: This movie theater at a ski resort shows Hollywood features on 2 screens & has stadium seating.

properties.name: Word after Word Books
properties.description: Word After Word Books is a welcoming space for the community and visitors to connect around books. We carry titles by local authors, regional and history books about the area, and books and magazines pertaining to the outdoor lifestyle in our community. We also have the latest bestsellers and favorite staff picks. There is a section for children and young adults, the Kids & Teens Corner, with comfy beanbag chairs. The Word After Word Team hosts author events, writing workshops, book clubs, and story times.

properties.name: Bear Yuba Land Trust
properties.description: Bear Yuba Land Trust is a private, non profit, membership supported organization that works to promote voluntary conservation of the region’s natural, historical and agricultural legacy. The Land Trust provides a lasting community heritage by protecting and enhancing meadows, watersheds, forests, farms and ranches, trails and parks. BYLT exists to create a balance between nature and the needs of people like you who make a life and a livelihood here. Our mission is to enrich your deep community connection with the land today, tomorrow, and forever.

properties.name: City of Grass Valley Historical Commission
properties.description: It is the intent of the City to encourage the preservation of the City's historical and architecturally significant resources by the formation of a Historical Commission which has the duty and function to identify and designate historical properties within the City and to honor those property owners who have maintained or restored these historical properties.

properties.name: Daughters of the American Revolution – Capt. John Oldham Chapter
properties.description: The DAR, founded in 1890 and headquartered in Washington, DC is a non profit, non-political, volunteer women's service organization dedicated to promoting patriotism, preserving American history, and securing America's future through better education for children.

properties.name: Historic Mount St. Mary’s Preservation Committee
properties.description: St. Joseph's Cultural Center is a nonprofit organization dedicated to preserving a historic landmark for cultural activities in the community. It is owned and operated by the Historic Mount. St. Mary's Preservation committee.

properties.name: Nevada County Gem & Mineral Society
properties.description: The Nevada County Gem and Mineral Society is a non-profit organization that has the following objectives: To promote the study of mineralogy, geology, and fossils. To encourage the collection of minerals and gems. To foster the study and practice of the lapidary arts. To provide field trips to mineral localities. To promote good fellowship, education and recreation.

properties.name: North Star Historic Conservancy
properties.description: The Conservancy is working to re establish the unique, historic North Star House and grounds into a premier cultural event center. It is committed to maintaining the property for the public by promoting tours, events educational activities and the arts.

properties.name: Gold Country Celtic Society
properties.description: The Gold Country Celtic Society was organized to celebrate the culture of these proud people, many of whom, especially the Cornish and Welsh, worked the hard rock mines and made our Nevada City Grass Valley area famous. The Society wishes to perpetuate Celtic traditions of music, dance,language, and costume.

properties.name: Nevada Cemetery District
properties.description: The Nevada Cemetery District is an independent special district charged with providing respectful and cost effective interment services appropriate for the diversity of local conditions and circumstances. It is responsible for the improvement, expansion and operation of public cemeteries in Western Nevada County, including historic cemeteries established by fraternal, pioneer, religious, social and other organizations

properties.name: Nevada City Rancheria of the Nisenan Tribe
properties.description: The Nevada City Rancheria was created from an executive order by President Woodrow Wilson in 1913 from a land allotment obtained by Chief Charlie Cully in 1887. Cully's land on Cement Hill became Federal Trust Land and the Tribal government became a federally recognized entity. The Nevada City Rancheria was one of 48 Rancherias illegally terminated in the 1950s and 1960s by the California Rancheria Termination Acts. Most have been restored; the Nevada City Nisenan Rancheria has not. Yet.

properties.name: Nevada County Cultural Preservation Trust
properties.description: The Miners Foundry Cultural Center is a living historic treasure serving Nevada County as a cultural arts and community events center. More than a venue, the nonprofit’s mission is to preserve, enhance and utilize the historic Miners Foundry for cultural, educational and social activities in Nevada County. It is a symbol of California’s rich historical heritage, providing an educational and social bridge from our past to the present and future.

properties.name: Nevada County Historical Society
properties.description: The Nevada County Historical Society had its beginning in the fall of 1944 after being inspired by Dr. Robert E. Burns, President of the College of the Pacific. The objective of the new organization was to preserve every source of Nevada County history. Monthly programs were planned to entertain and educate members, and the committee chairmen and groups formed the foundation of the society by collecting, displaying, and preserving the memorabilia of the county.

properties.name: Nevada Theatre Commission
properties.description: The Nevada Theatre Commission is a group of volunteers who are dedicated to making sure the Nevada Theatre continues to be available and usable to our community for years to come. The Nevada Theatre is owned and maintained by the the Commission which is a non profit 501(c)(3) organization.

properties.name: Ngakmang Foundation
properties.description: To preserve the spiritual, medical, and cultural traditions of the Himalayan and Tibetan region.

properties.name: South Yuba River Citizens League
properties.description: SYRCL unites the community to protect and restore the Yuba River. Motivated by our love for this watershed, we advocate powerfully, engage in active stewardship, educate the public, and inspire activism from the Sierra to the sea.

properties.name: The Sierra Fund
properties.description: The Sierra Fund’s mission is to restore ecosystem and community resiliency in the Sierra Nevada.

properties.name: California Heritage Indigenous Research Project
properties.description: CALIFORNIA HERITAGE: INDIGENOUS RESEARCH PROJECT (CHIRP) was created to research, document, preserve, and protect California Indigenous Nisenan culture. CHIRP continues to support the Nisenan in their quest to reestablish themselves as the Indigenous people in the foothills where their families have resided for thousands of years. CHIRP's area of influence is mainly the counties of Sierra, Yuba, Sutter, Nevada, Placer and Butte.

properties.name: Famous Marching Presidents Inc.
properties.description: The Famous Marching Presidents organization honors the U.S. Constitution in the involvement of the Nevada City Constitution Day Parade and other related public celebrations.

properties.name: Donner Summit Historical Society
properties.description: Donner Summit Historical Society is dedicated to telling the story of the most important historical square mile in California and maybe the entire Western U.S. We operate a museum on the Summit as well as the 20 Mile Museum along Old Highway 40.

properties.name: C A T T Community Project
properties.description: CATT Community Project is a 501c(3) non profit, charitable organization established in October 1999 as a sponsored arm of the Contractors Association of Truckee Tahoe. CATT Community Project is governed by a 10 member Board of Directors which meets monthly. CATT Community Project provides design and construction assistance to other non profits and governmental agencies as a way of giving back to the community.

properties.name: Mountain Area Preservation Foundation
properties.description: To preserve the Truckee Tahoe region’s mountain character and natural environment for present and future generations.

properties.name: Truckee Donner Historical Society
properties.description: Truckee Donner Historical Society was founded July 24, 1968 to preserve the history of the Truckee Donner area. Besides maintaining the historic Joseph Research Cabin (the Gateway Cabin), the Society is also responsible for operating the Old Jail Museum which was built in 1875. The Society maintains the Community Memorial Garden including the Memorial Arch behind the Old Jail Museum, the Donner Cross, and Schaffer Cemetery.

properties.name: Truckee Donner Horsemen
properties.description: Truckee Donner Horsemen is a non-profit organization of people who love horses. We are based in Truckee, CA in the high Sierra mountains. Our Mission is to Promote Horsemanship and Preserve the McIver Arena

properties.name: Truckee Donner Railroad Society
properties.description: The mission of the Truckee Donner Railroad Society is to perpetuate the study and advancement of knowledge of working railroads in the Truckee Donner area with the goal of building a railroad museum to preserve and share what we have learned.

properties.description: Artist: Miles Toland. For Suicide awareness.

properties.name: The Curious Salmon Bike Rack
properties.description: Made and installed by Curious Forge. Description: Custom built bike rack in the shape of 5 salmon arching out of the pavement. Evolution: Amy and Chamba Cooke (owners's Summer Thyme's) approached Liam Ellerby at the Curious Forge with the idea of a bike rack as an art piece. The piece intends to raise awareness of the salmon in our watershed, foster bicycle use as green transportation, and encourage the enjoyment of art as practical and useful.

properties.description: Artist: Sally Peterson

properties.description: Artist: Sally Peterson

properties.description: Donated by Adelaide Tibessart Plaque reads: "Pelton water wheel. Donated by Adelaide Tibessart in memory of her father, Thomas N. Coan, miner and pioneer of Indian Flat.

properties.name: Stained Glass -- Lower waiting area lobby, Sierra Nevada Memorial Hospital
properties.description: Artist: Pamela Mengers Hodges In chapel which is off the hallway when you come into the main entrance.

properties.name: Fountain sculpture at Pioneer Park
properties.description: Design: Francis Lloyd, Sculptor: Norman Grag. The fountain was installed in 1976 with funds from the John J. Looser estate. The fountain was designed by architect Francis Lloyd and the metal sculpture was created by artist Norman Grag. The fountain was placed in the middle of an existing city street that ran through the park. The placement of the fountain resulted in an intentional redirection of the road.

properties.name: Mural at Pioneer Park swimming pool
properties.description: Artist: Jordan Mal did the project in 2016 as her Senior Project. Mural on the wall at the swimming pool -- serves as a painting and safety signage. The wave on the wall matches the depth of the swimming pool, so if a child stands by the wall and the wave is over their head, they know that if they walk straight from that spot into the poolду_the water will be over their head.

properties.name: Robinson Plaza -- Mining sculptures: Pelton Water Wheel and Five Stamp Mill
properties.description: Pelton Water Wheel: Gift from PGE and Nevada City, dedicated Sept 17 1987. Pelton Water Wheel was in continuous use at PGE Drum Power House No. 4 from 1928-1987. 12 ft diameter, 15 tons. Five-Stamp Mill: (1893) used to crush gold ore at Fortuna Mine near Nevada City. Donated to Nevada City by Bill Magers. Dedicated July 4, 1982

properties.name: Calanan Park -- Metal sculpture
properties.description: Artist: Miriam Morris. Like Robinson Plaza, a metal sculpture designed to block electrical box.

properties.name: Chinese Monument
properties.description: The Chinese Monument consists of a large rock, babbling fountain and dual plaques in English and old Chinese characters, all surrounded by natural landscaping.

properties.name: Ma Trux"" Mural at Mother Truckers
properties.description: Artist: David Randolf Griffis. David says ""I painted the ma trux mural 16 years ago for a roast beef sandwich."" Mother Trucker's is like ""the town square of the ridge.""

properties.description: Artist: Grace Mack, Gem Visuals. Completed September 2016

properties.description: Artist: Grace Mack, Gem Visuals. First two sides completed August 2017. Third wall completed May 2018

properties.name: Life Cycle of the Monarch"" mural project
properties.description: Concept: Avani Patrick. Four images designed by Grace Mack Gem Visuals, then transferred (with help from Stephanie Hubbard and Avani Patrick) to prepared plywood. The whole community was invited to come together to paint it.

properties.name: The Community Center Sign
properties.description: Artist: Grace Mack, Gem Visuals. Hand painted, two sided, 40 sq ft. Both faces of the sign (about 20 sq ft each) were individually hand painted over the spring and summer, then set into this frame by Avani Patrick, Nikko and team.

properties.name: Iron sculpture -- Ridge Cafн© sign
properties.description: Artist: Eric Clausen. An iron sculpture/sign at The Ridge Cafe. A cafe frequented by artists, farmers, craftsmen, tradesmen, mountain folk, neighbors, travelers, out-door enthusiasts, health-nuts, hippies & hillbillies.

properties.name: Paintings at Penn Valley Chamber of Commerce
properties.description: Three paintings by local artist Curre hang at the Chamber.

properties.name: DeRailed"" bike rack
properties.description: Artist: Bernie LaForest of Striker Forge. ''DeRailed'' which is part of the Truckee Artistic Bike Rack Project by TPAC, is made of repurposed rail steel. Created by artist Bernie LaForest of Striker Forge, it represents the railRd. and its role bringing commerce to California. It is fittingly placed in the plaza across from Truckee Train Station and was commissioned by the Town of Truckee.

properties.name: High Iron"" bike rack.
properties.description: Artist: Bernie LaForest, Striker Forge. ''High Iron'' by Bernie LaForest of Striker Forge is the most vertical in the Truckee Artistic Bike Rack project. The sculpture is made of rail steel and was commissioned by the Town of Truckee.

properties.name: Locomotion"" bike sculpture
properties.description: Artist: Fred Besch. ''Locomotion'' was created by local kinetic artist Fred Besch. The giant bike sculpture can be found in the plaza west of the historic Flying A building. The sculpture represents residents' love of biking. The art shares plaza space with a giant chess board that was created and donated by Truckee Roundhouse Makerspace.

properties.name: Martis Sculpture"" - front of Martis Outlook professional building.
properties.description: Artist: Anton Standteiner, Mountain Forge. ''Martis Sculpture'' was created by artist Anton Standteiner of The Mountain Forge and commissioned by the Town of Truckee.The flow and style of the sculpture honors the Native American people who once inhabited Martis Valley. It stands in front of the Martis Outlook professional building on the corner of Soaring Way and Truckee Tahoe Airport Way.

properties.name: Mountain Flowers"" sculpture
properties.description: Artist: Troy Corliss, Gallery 5830'. Mountain Flowers sculpture stands 16-18 feet high in the roundabout across from the Truckee Donner Community Center on Donner Pass Rd.. The sculpture was a gift to the Town of Truckee from artist Troy Corliss of Gallery 5830'. Other contributors, in services or supplies, were The Mountain Forge, JMC Industrial Finishing, Huldrege & Kull, and Perennial Landscape & Nursery.

properties.name: The Band""
properties.description: Designed by Anton Standteiner, this metal sculpture suggests the flow and movement of music.

properties.name: The Feather"" bike rack project.
properties.description: Artist: Lauren Bello Okerman. ''The Feather'' is part of the Truckee Artistic Bike Rack Project by TPAC. It was designed by Lauren Bello Okerman and is made of powder coated steel. The art serves as a bike rack in front of the Truckee Donner Recreation & Park District. This sculpture was sponsored by the Queen of Hearts Women's Fund.

properties.name: Animal Tracks"" -- bike rack project. At Tahoe Forest Hospital.
properties.description: Artist: Kevin Kremler. ''Animal Tracks'' is a painted steel bike rack sculpture featuring paw prints of local animals. It was made by artist Kevin Kremler and is part of the TPAC Truckee Artistic Bike Rack Project. It was commissioned by Jae Gustafson for Tahoe Forest Hospital.

properties.name: Community As Ecosystem""
properties.description: Artists: Sara L. Smith, Toni Standteiner/Mountain Forge. Commissioned by the TRDRPD, 'думCommunity As Ecosystem" main mural image measures more than 11 ft. high by 16 ft. wide. The piece celebrates Truckee's new Aquatic Center donors. The artists represent the concept of joyful balance noting each donor (no matter level of contribution) is a vital part of the overall effort. The mural speaks to the wider concept of community as an ecosystem, and reminds us that we are all essential. The artists note: "The mural is painted using sepia tones to tie us back to our past, and is painted upon a wall created with reclaimed Lake Tahoe pier wood, creating a tangible tie to our physical past and present. It depicts a stylized image of Martis Valleyду»s diverse natural environment, and donors are represented by a combination of cut and manipulated animal shapes and forged iron environmental elements, such as mountain peaks, cattails and wave forms in the creek. On a separate panel each and every donorду»s name has been hand lettered, grouped next to its corresponding donor category and animal shape.дуќ In addition two additional small panels were created to specifically recognize the individuals who helped shepherd the project to fruition, and well as the organizations that contributed multi-million dollar grant funds.

properties.name: Aspen Grove"" in Radiation Lobby at Tahoe Forest Cancer Center
properties.description: Artist: Troy Corliss. The colorful array of glass leaves in the Troy Corliss думAspen Groveдуќ welcomes patients and visitors to the Radiation Therapy waiting space. Fabricated in steel and kiln fused glass, the artwork integrates an experience of the natural world with interior architecture. The gentle curves of the tree trunks and the subtle patterning and warm colors of the glass leaves creates a three dimensional design that is both intriguing and calming. The Grove can be experienced as a powerful whole at first sight or explored with interest with repeated visits.
